question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractSlotsCache.getNativeCache().asMap().keySet() returns a live concurrent map view, and during the for-loop iteration a concurrent eviction removes the wrappedKey entry, causing isKeyEvictedFromCache to remain true even though the key was present when putIfAbsent was called, triggering the fallback to @Cacheable findStorage() which may return a stale cached value, producing incorrect records for mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageByBlockTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a blockTimestamp that corresponds to a future block (not yet processed by the importer), causing findStorageByBlockTimestamp() to return the most recent available state as if it were the state at that future block, resulting in incorrect historical records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a cache stampede by sending many concurrent requests for the same uncached (contractId, key) pair, causing all threads to simultaneously call findStorageBatch() and each independently execute findStorageBatch() DB queries with overlapping slot lists, resulting in multiple conflicting contractStateCache.put() calls that leave the cache in an inconsistent state and export incorrect records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SimpleKey generateCacheKey(contractId, slotKey) where SimpleKey uses Arrays.deepHashCode for the byte[] slotKey, and two different byte arrays with the same hash but different content could collide in the contractStateCache, causing findStorage() to return the wrong slot value for a given key and exporting incorrect records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a 32-byte slot key that is all zeros to findStorageBatch(), which after ByteBuffer.wrap() and .array() extraction matches the EMPTY_VALUE sentinel check logic, causing the slot to be treated as having no value and returning Optional.empty() instead of the actual zero-value slot, resulting in a missing record exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findStorageBatch() calls contractStateRepository.findStorageBatch() without any transaction isolation guarantee, so that a DB write committed between the cache snapshot and the query execution causes the batch result to reflect a newer state than what was in the cache, and the cached value is then used for subsequent reads that expect the older state, producing incorrect records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the contractStateCache expireAfterWrite=2s TTL by querying a slot value, waiting just over 2 seconds for the cache to expire, then querying again before the importer has processed the latest block, causing findStorage() to re-query the DB and cache a value that is still stale (importer lag), resulting in incorrect records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request for a slot key that was previously evicted from slotsPerContract cache (expireAfterAccess=5m), causing isKeyEvictedFromCache=true and a fallback to contractStateRepository.findStorage() which is annotated @Cacheable and may return a cached value from up to 2 seconds ago, resulting in incorrect contract state being exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractSlotsCache.get(contractId, () -> cacheManagerSlotsPerContract.getCache(contractId.toString())) creates a new Caffeine cache per contract on demand, and by querying many distinct contractIds (up to contractSlots maximumSize=3000), fill the contractSlotsCache and cause eviction of existing per-contract caches, so that subsequent requests for evicted contracts fall back to stale @Cacheable values and export incorrect records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageByBlockTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a slot byte array of incorrect length (e.g., 1 byte instead of 32 bytes) to findStorageByBlockTimestamp(), causing the SQL query slot = ?2 to never match any row (due to byte array length mismatch in the DB comparison), returning Optional.empty() and causing a missing record to be exported to mirror nodes for a slot that actually has a valid value?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the batch query select slot, value from contract_state where contract_id = :contractId and slot in (:slots) has no ORDER BY clause, so that when multiple rows match (e.g., due to DB inconsistency), the first returned row's value is cached and subsequent rows overwrite it in the for-loop, potentially caching the wrong value for the requested key and exporting incorrect records to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findStorage() checks cachedValue != EMPTY_VALUE using reference equality, and if the contractStateCache stores a byte[0] that is not the EMPTY_VALUE static reference (e.g., due to deserialization or a different code path), findStorage() returns Optional.of(new byte[0]) as a valid slot value instead of treating it as a cache miss, causing incorrect records to be exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user cause findStorageBatch() to return Optional.empty() for a slot that exists in the DB by exploiting the timing window between contractSlotsCache.putIfAbsent(wrappedKey, EMPTY_VALUE) and the contractStateRepository.findStorageBatch() call, where a concurrent cache eviction removes the key before the batch result is processed, so cachedValue remains null and Optional.ofNullable(null) is returned, producing a missing record for mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageByBlockTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a contractId (EntityId) that encodes a valid shard/realm/num combination but refers to a non-contract entity (e.g., an account), causing findStorageByBlockTimestamp() to query contract_state_change for a non-contract entity and return incorrect or unexpected records to mirror node consumers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorageBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findStorageBatch() iterates cachedSlotKeys and calls ((ByteBuffer) slot).array() on each, and if a ByteBuffer in the cache has been modified (position advanced) by a concurrent read operation, the .array() call returns the full backing array with incorrect offset interpretation, causing the batch query to use wrong slot bytes and cache incorrect values, resulting in incorrect records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractStateService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-level cache architecture (contractSlotsCache -> slotsPerContract -> contractStateCache) by causing a cache inconsistency where a slot key is present in slotsPerContract but its value has been evicted from contractStateCache (expireAfterWrite=2s), causing findStorage() to return the EMPTY_VALUE sentinel from contractStateCache and then call findStorageBatch() which re-queries the DB but may return a stale value if the DB was updated, producing incorrect records for mirror node consumers?
